Energies.narrative: Crude oil markets shifted decisively overnight, with the $100/bbl by end-of-March contract falling 10.7pp to 72.2% on $2.2M volume — the largest liquid energy move of the session. Natural gas probability structure repriced sharply across the board: the $4.00 threshold contract dropped 12pp to 55%, while the $4.04 level fell 14pp to 19%, suggesting the market is compressing its near-term nat gas price distribution lower despite the European storage crisis. The Iran intervention probability also slid 7.5pp to 9% on nearly $6.8M in volume, indicating reduced near-term risk of a ground escalation even as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

Rates.narrative: Rates markets registered modest but directionally consistent moves. Powell's tenure odds gained 12pp to 36% on $28K volume, reflecting renewed speculation about a leadership change at the Fed as stagflation risk rises. The June 2026 25bps cut probability ticked up 4pp to 12%, signaling very limited near-term easing expectations despite the oil shock. An inflation surge in 2026 contract trades near parity at 54.8% (up 4.8pp), consistent with the upward inflation revision to 2.7% and the market's reluctance to price in dovish Fed pivot.

Equities.narrative: Equities prediction markets showed thin liquidity with mixed signals. The SpaceX IPO probability dropped 10pp to 22% on $28.5K volume, suggesting the market is pushing out IPO timing expectations amid the uncertain macro environment. The S&P 500 sub-6000 by Jan 2027 contract gained 7pp to 80%, reinforcing the bearish near-term equity narrative consistent with the index being down 8% from February highs. H100 GPU compute pricing odds moved up 17pp to 19% on thin volume ($1.3K), a potential early signal of AI infrastructure demand repricing.

Crypto.narrative: Crypto markets staged a notable risk-off reversal with BTC downside protection rapidly repricing off highs. The BTC dip to $65K in March contract collapsed 27.5pp to 46.2% on $1.58M volume — the single largest liquid crypto move — as BTC held above that level. The $65K trimmed mean by March 31 contract similarly fell 25pp to 49%, while the more extreme $62.5K level dropped 17pp to 20%, suggesting markets are pricing out the tail scenario of a sharp end-of-month breakdown. SOL downside odds also eased, with the sub-$80 trimmed mean contract falling 20pp to 53%.

Geopolitics.narrative: The Iran conflict resolution odds saw the sharpest move of the session: the probability of an Iranian military action against Israel on March 23 collapsed 28.1pp to just 0.9% on $315K volume, likely resolving as the date passed without incident. US ground forces entering Iran by March 31 fell 7.5pp to 9% on the largest geopolitical market by volume ($6.8M), signaling reduced near-term escalation risk. Concurrently, Trump visiting China by May 31 gained 7pp to 79% on $247K volume, and a Trump-Xi meeting before April 1 rose 12pp to 28% — markets are increasingly pricing a diplomatic track alongside the military posturing.

Commodities.narrative: Crude oil probability structure shifted meaningfully with the $100/bbl by end-of-March contract falling 10.7pp to 72.2% on $2.2M volume, suggesting market participants are trimming the probability of an imminent $100 print despite Brent already at $120 and the Hormuz closure. The $110 level fell only 1.8pp to 22.4%, while $105 rose 1.7pp to 45.2%, indicating the distribution is compressing around the $100-$110 range. A U.S. credit rating downgrade contract spiked 14.9pp to 50% on $10.6K volume — a commodities-adjacent macro risk that could further accelerate safe-haven gold flows.

Macro.narrative: Gas price markets dominated macro movers with a broad downward reprice across near-term thresholds: the $4.00 average gas price contract fell 12pp to 55% on $101K volume, and $4.04 dropped 14pp to 19% on $11K. The $3.96 floor contract rose 8pp to 95%, compressing the probability distribution toward a $3.96-$4.00 range for near-term average gas prices. Japan's unemployment rate contract for February 2026 saw a 15pp upward move to 50% on $1.6K volume — thin but directionally consistent with global labor market softening concerns as the oil shock propagates through supply chains.

Elections.narrative: US government shutdown markets saw the most significant liquid moves: the probability of a shutdown lasting at least 70 days (from Feb 7) surged 29pp to 66% on $120K volume, while DHS funding becoming law before April 1 collapsed 30pp to 1% on $21.7K volume — together signaling near-certainty of a prolonged funding gap. The DoE elimination legislation contract fell 43pp to 6% on $39K volume, suggesting a key administration priority is losing Congressional support. Immigration enforcement disclosure legislation rose sharply, with two related contracts moving to 78% and 91% respectively, reflecting active legislative momentum on that front.

Sports.narrative: Sports markets were dominated by in-progress or recently resolved events. The most notable liquid mover with genuine informational content was the Texas Children's Houston Open, where Sudarshan Yellamaraju and Nicolai Hojgaard both saw their top-20/top-5 finish probabilities surge 59pp on volumes of $35K and $3.5K respectively, consistent with strong tournament positioning. The Counter-Strike BLAST Open Rotterdam contract (Natus Vincere vs PARIVISION) moved to near-certainty at 100% on $1.19M volume, suggesting a decisive series result. NCAA March Madness brackets are resolving with Providence vs Marquette and Notre Dame vs Virginia showing clean winner resolutions.
